name: Wren Looseweft
voicePatterns: dry humor — treats impossible situations as craft problems
forge metaphors — 'You don't curse the metal for bending. You adjust the technique.'
no patience for self-pity — cuts through emotional noise
speaks about Fray transformation as material property change, not tragedy
dialogueHooks: explaining how she forges with Fray-touched materials
organizing salvage runs into frayed zones
providing the Hollow Court perspective on the Fray as resource, not curse
offering to craft Thread-Iron tools if the player brings materials

always: Hollow Court settlement operations and survival techniques
which frayed zones are stable enough to salvage
practical effects of Fray transformation on people and materials
Thread-Iron crafting basics — working metal infused with Fray energy
sometimes: the Hollow Court's political relationship with other factions
specific properties of Fray-touched materials and their applications
locations of valuable salvage in the frayed zones
rarely: she has nascent pattern sight — she sees weave structure through forge fire
forge-fire follows Threadlines when she works near them
she's developing Thread-Iron techniques that could revolutionize ward maintenance

morning.activity: stoking the forge, planning the day's work

night.activity: sketching designs, experimenting with Fray-metal alloys

backstory: Wren was a village blacksmith before the Fray took her settlement. She didn't flee — she adapted. When her skin began to refract light strangely and her eyes shifted, she treated it the way she'd treat any material change: observe, adjust technique, keep working. She organized the survivors, found a stable zone in the Frayed territory, and built Hollowreach through sheer organizational will. She's the Hollow Court's de facto leader not by election but by being the person who got things done when no one else could.
secret: Wren has nascent pattern sight — the rarest form of Fray-touch. She sees weave structure through forge-fire, and the fire follows Threadlines when she works near them. She doesn't call it 'sight' — she calls it 'reading the grain.' Her Thread-Iron techniques are the beginning of something revolutionary.
motivation: Build a future for the Fray-touched that doesn't depend on anyone's pity or permission
tensions: de facto leader who never wanted to lead — she wanted to forge
pragmatic to a fault — sometimes dismisses emotional needs as weakness
possesses an extraordinary gift she refuses to call magical

knownFacts: Glasseye sees things no one else can
Glasseye's quiet observations have saved lives
glasseye.history: Wren trusts Glasseye's perception implicitly. They complement each other — she builds, he sees.

knownFacts: Farweft is more open-minded than most walkers
farweft.history: Cautious respect. Farweft doesn't flinch around the Fray-touched, which Wren appreciates.
